sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen, sitting in 2nd place with 68 points, welcome FC Augsburg, who are 11th with 43 points, to the BayArena in Leverkusen for Round 31 of the Bundesliga. Leverkusen have been in scintillating form this season, boasting the 2nd highest goal tally in the league with 68 goals scored. Meanwhile, Augsburg have struggled to keep pace, finding themselves in the middle of the table. This matchup at the BayArena promises to be a test of Leverkusen’s attacking prowess against a side looking to spoil their momentum. The referee for the game, Christian Dingert, averages 4.3 yellow cards and 0.19 red cards per game, indicating a likelihood of a few cautions during the encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Bayer 04 Leverkusen have dominated with 3 wins, while FC Augsburg managed 2 victories, with no draws recorded. In their most recent clash on December 14, 2024, Leverkusen secured a 2-0 victory away at Augsburg, with Florian Wirtz earning the Player of the Match title with an impressive 8.1 Sofascore rating. Leverkusen have been the first to score in 5 of their last 7 head-to-head encounters, a trend that bodes well for the home side. Additionally, matches between these teams have often seen less than 4.5 cards in 5 of the last 7 games, suggesting a relatively clean contest might be on the cards.

sections.subtitle: Leverkusen’s Key Performers
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen are loaded with talent, particularly in attack, with Patrik Schick leading the charge as the 2nd highest goalscorer in the league with 19 goals. Florian Wirtz, another standout, ranks 2nd in assists with 10 and 5th in average rating at 7.68, while also sitting 3rd for successful dribbles per game at 2.7 and 2nd for big chances created with 16. Alejandro Grimaldo has been pivotal in midfield, ranking 3rd league-wide for key passes with 71. Defensively, Jonathan Tah excels with a league 2nd ranking for aerial duels won at 75.8%. Other contributors like Jeremie Frimpong and Granit Xhaka, with 5 and 7 assists respectively, add depth to Leverkusen’s dynamic lineup.

sections.subtitle: Augsburg’s Notable Players
sections.text: FC Augsburg will rely on their forward line to challenge Leverkusen’s defense, with Alexis Claude-Maurice leading the way with 9 goals this season. Samuel Essende and Phillip Tietz have chipped in with 7 and 6 goals respectively, providing some threat upfront. Defensively, Keven Schlotterbeck stands out with a 3rd league ranking for ground duels won at 71.7%, while Chrislain Matsima ranks 5th for aerial duels won at 70.3%. Midfielder Frank Onyeka, often fouled, ranks 2nd in the league with 2.5 fouls drawn per game, which could disrupt Leverkusen’s rhythm if they’re not cautious.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Leverkusen are currently on a streak of 3 games without a loss, showcasing their resilience at home. They’ve also been the first to score in 4 of their last 5 matches, underlining their ability to take control early. On the other hand, Augsburg have seen less than 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 away games, suggesting a potentially low-scoring affair from their perspective. Augsburg have also seen less than 4.5 cards in their last 5 away games, aligning with their head-to-head trend. Leverkusen’s matches have resulted in less than 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 home games, which could indicate a game with fewer set-piece opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Leverkusen’s Tactical Strengths
sections.text: Leverkusen’s season statistics highlight their dominance, with the 2nd best ball possession in the league at 59.59% and ranking 2nd for accurate passes at 87.24%. They’ve created 87 big chances, ranking 5th, and converted 45.98% of them, placing them 2nd in the league for efficiency. Their ability to win possessions in the final third per game is unmatched, ranking 1st with 5.16, while penalty area entries per game stand at 32.09, also 2nd in the league. Their goal distribution shows strength late in games, with 27.03% of goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Players like Piero Hincapié, in form with a 7.76 average rating over the last 5 matches, will be key to maintaining defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Augsburg’s Challenges
sections.text: Augsburg face an uphill battle against Leverkusen’s high-pressing game, especially considering their head-to-head streak of failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 encounters against the hosts. Their goal distribution shows a balanced spread, with 22.22% of goals scored in both the 1st-15th and 31st-45th minute periods, but they lack the firepower to match Leverkusen’s output. Finn Dahmen, their goalkeeper, is in form with a 7.5 average rating over the last 5 matches, and he’ll need to be at his best to keep Leverkusen’s attackers like Schick and Wirtz at bay. Augsburg’s defenders like Jeffrey Gouweleeuw will also need to step up to contain Leverkusen’s relentless attacks.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Standout Performers
sections.text: Leverkusen’s Jonas Hofmann has been a standout in the last 5 matchups against Augsburg, averaging an 8.1 Sofascore rating, scoring 2 goals, and providing 1 assist across 5 starts. Leverkusen rank 2nd in the league for corners per game with 6.28, which could lead to set-piece opportunities against Augsburg. They’ve also hit the woodwork 19 times this season, ranking 2nd for unluckiest in the league, showing they’ve been close to scoring even more. Augsburg’s reliance on physicality might see players like Frank Onyeka draw fouls, potentially leading to free kicks in dangerous areas. Leverkusen’s missing forward Martin Terrier and defender Nordi Mukiele might slightly dent their depth, but their squad remains formidable.
